own_review.issue: gluonhq/setup-graalvm pinned to mutable @master (with GITHUB_TOKEN passed in env) instead of a tag/SHA; breaks repo's pinning convention, supply-chain/reproducibility risk

GUI jobs lose Maven dependency caching: the old graalvm/setup-graalvm step had cache:'maven' for all modes; it is now gated to CLI only and the new Gluon setup step configures no cache, so GUI builds re-download all deps

Dev docker base still installs Oracle GraalVM (download.oracle.com/graalvm/23/latest) while the PR's own premise is that GUI native builds fail on non-Gluon GraalVM 23 (CI GUI switched to Gluon's build) — the docker-compose dev GUI build likely remains broken; also the /latest/ URL for a non-LTS release will 404 once 23 is archived
Docs still instruct contributors to install Java 21 / GraalVM 21; PR leaves documentation checklist unchecked
Inconsistent arch values passed to the same gluonhq/setup-graalvm input; per the human maintainer the arch parameter is unnecessary since Docker images are amd64-only

File rewritten onto a single line (kills diffability); new top-level 'globs' array duplicates the retained legacy 'resources.includes' but drifts from it: globs omit SplashLight.png, DrawRoundRect_Color.frag, Blend_SRC_IN.frag, LinearConvolveShadow_4/_20.frag and icudt72b entries; bundles dropped the root "" locale. Legacy includes are retained so runtime impact is unlikely, but the dual-format duplication invites drift

New 1807-line agent-generated reachability-metadata.json added while legacy reflect-config.json (521 lines) and jni-config.json (123 lines) are kept — same metadata in two formats, both auto-discovered by native-image; legacy files should be removed or regenerated together
Upgrade targets non-LTS JDK 23 (6-month support window) from LTS 21; Windows/macOS GUI executables explicitly untested per PR description

points.point: Stale old-format reflect-config.json (521 lines) / jni-config.json (123 lines) not removed alongside new reachability-metadata.json
points.note: Verified: both files exist at base with exactly those line counts; duplication across formats is real

points.point: Shader fragments DrawRoundRect_Color/LinearConvolveShadow_20/Blend_SRC_IN missing from new globs array
points.note: Verified absent from globs (also _4 and SplashLight.png, unmentioned); runtime-risk framing overstated because the legacy includes section retaining them is kept, but point is hedged and the inconsistency is real

points.point: Bundles lost empty-string default locale ("" removed from locales)
points.note: Verified for QuantumMessagesBundle, XMLEntities and controls bundles

points.point: Windows GluonFX override comment lacks external issue/release-notes link

points.note: Verified in both build.yml and docker-publish.yml

points.point: CodeQL uses Oracle JDK rather than GraalVM
points.note: distribution:'oracle' is pre-existing and acceptable, as the point itself concedes

missed: Human point: arch parameter in docker-publish.yml is unnecessary (Docker images are amd64-only); also 'amd64' vs 'x86_64' inconsistency with build.yml
Docs not updated: Quickstart.md and Building-Executables.md still say Java 21 / GraalVM 21
GUI CI jobs lose Maven dependency caching (cache:'maven' now CLI-only; Gluon setup step has no cache)
docker-compose dev base still uses Oracle GraalVM 23 although the PR's premise is GUI builds need Gluon's GraalVM — dev GUI build likely still broken
exceeds: Stale reflect-config.json/jni-config.json duplication (not in human reference)

points.point: GLSL shaders missing from new globs (lists DrawRoundRect_Color, FillRoundRect_Color, Blend_SRC_IN, LinearConvolveShadow_4, LinearConvolveShadow_20)
points.note: 4 of 5 verified missing from globs, but FillRoundRect_Color.frag IS present in the new globs — one fabricated absence; also says 'four' then lists five. Risk framing also ignores that legacy includes are retained

points.point: Bundle locales lost empty-string default locale
gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master unpinned vs repo pinning convention (claims 49+ pinned refs)
points.note: Issue verified; actual pinned count is ~44-46 and three @main refs already exist, so the convention stat is slightly inflated but the substance holds

points.point: JavaFX dependencies at 23-ea+20 (Early Access) in pom.xml:30-35 should move to stable for a Java 23 GA upgrade
points.note: Verified at base pom.xml lines 30/35; pre-existing but directly pertinent to this upgrade PR — good use of repo context

points.point: Windows profile hardcodes gluonfx 1.0.23 — noted, no action needed

points.note: Entries do exist in the file, but they are not Java-23-specific APIs (records: 16+, ModuleLayer: 9) — soft mischaracterization in a praise point
Praise overlooks that the dev base remains on Oracle GraalVM while CI GUI had to switch to Gluon's build

points.point: Compiler source/target upgrades consistent across all POMs

missed: Human point: arch parameter in docker-publish.yml is unnecessary (Docker images are amd64-only); also 'amd64' vs 'x86_64' inconsistency with build.yml
Docs not updated: Quickstart.md and Building-Executables.md still say Java 21 / GraalVM 21
GUI CI jobs lose Maven dependency caching (cache:'maven' now CLI-only; Gluon setup step has no cache)
docker-compose dev base still uses Oracle GraalVM 23 although the PR's premise is GUI builds need Gluon's GraalVM — v6 even blessed this file as 'correct'
exceeds: JavaFX 23-ea+20 pre-existing EA dependency (repo-context finding beyond the diff)

qualitative: Both reviews are substantive and land four verified non-trivial findings each, far exceeding the single-point human reference, yet both miss the same cluster of real issues: the maintainer's actual comment (unnecessary/inconsistent arch param in docker-publish.yml), stale Java-21 contributor docs, the loss of Maven caching for GUI CI jobs, and the dev docker-compose environment still running Oracle GraalVM that the PR itself implies cannot build the GUI. v5 is the more careful text — its line counts (521/123/1807) are exactly right and it has zero fabrications, though it pads with three trivial process nits. v6 shows better repo-grounding (the JavaFX 23-ea+20 finding is genuinely useful and beyond the diff) but is sloppier: it fabricates the absence of FillRoundRect_Color.frag which is present in the new globs, says 'four' while listing five shaders, inflates the pinned-action count, mislabels record/ModuleLayer reflection entries as 'Java 23-specific', and actively blesses docker-compose as 'correct' where a real inconsistency hides. Net scores tie at 0; v5 wins on precision, v6 on reach.
